summary:
To ensure effective force distribution and predictable outcomes, an indirect retainer must be positioned near the direct retainer; this aligns with established clinical practices emphasizing proximity-based anchorage strategies. Sentence two emphasizes localizing support through strategic positioning relative to the primary retention mechanism used in therapy. ```plaintext
To achieve optimal force transfer and stable results, indirect retainers should be placed near the direct retainer as per clinical best practices focusing on proximal anchorage benefits. Strategic localization enhances control over tooth movements and supports successful orthodontic maintenance.
```
